rulings.Case Title: ATLANTIC CREDIT & FINANCE INC. VS. JOHN W. INGLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of And Post Judgment Mtns To Compel Written Response And Production Of Documents And Esi, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 11 2013, Line 1, ASSIGNEE MIDLAND FUNDING LLC's (Post Judgment) Motion To Compel Written Response And Production Of Documents And ESI, And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the unopposed motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VENKANTA PULAKANTI MD VS. THE MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 11, 2013, Line 10, Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate The Petition is Denied. On the basis of its independent review of the record, the Court concludes that Respondent's findings are supported by the weight of the evidence. The records of Patient JH are admissible as business records in light of P's testimony that they were true and correct and his medical assistant's testimony concerning the office practice in preparing them. Respondent properly imposed discipline for dishonesty and altering medical records with fraudulent intent as to Patient VP. The evidence, including Petitioner's admissions, establishes that he knowingly falsified those records in order to influence the Board and its review. The evidence supports the inference that he knew his entries were false, but at minimum, he entered information he did not know was true. Respondent correctly found that TP was a patient at the time of the offending telephone call. The first part of the call is clearly that of a patient consulting with her physician and receiving medical advice. The parties' stipulation did not exclude TP continuing to be a patient after April 4, 2008. Given the factual circumstances, the penalty imposed was within the Board's discretion. Respondent is directed to prepare a statement of decision consistent with this ruling and responsive to Petitioner's issues to be addressed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: ALVARO SAUL MIGUEL VS. ADVANCED ENTERPRISES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 11, 2013, Line 13, Defendant Christison Company Inc., The Glassworks II Homeowners Association's Motion for Summary Judgment The motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff, as an employee of a subcontractor, cannot establish that defendant owed him a duty of care as a matter of law under Privette v. Sup. Crt. (1993) 5 Cal.4th 689 and Seabright Ins. Co. v. U.S. Airways (2011) 52 Cal.4th 590. There is no evidence that defendant negligently retained control of the work or engaged in any other negligence that affirmatively contributed to the injury. Under Seabright, defendant delegated any obligations it had under the OSHA regulations to plaintiff's employer. There is also no evidence that defendant concealed a defect on the property that contributed to the injury that plaintiff's employer could not discover. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and which complies with the requirements of CCP section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.= (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: TERRENCE BARNUM VS. PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder Of Deft Peter Heelan Nrtc Of Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 11, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT PETER HEELAN'S Joinder Of Deft Peter Heelan Ntc Of Motion To Compel Arbitration Denied. The arbitration agreement is procedurally unconscionable because it is a contract of adhesion and because the rules that would govern the arbitration were not provided. The arbitration agreement is substantively unconscionable because the exception for injunctive and similar equitable relief operates in favor of employers and goes far beyond merely acknowledging the statutory availability of interim relief. The Court declines to sever the term because it is integral to the agreement the parties reached concerning the scope of what would be arbitrated, and its severance would impose on the parties obligations to which they did not agree. This is true even though injunctive relief is not at issue in this case because an employer cannot impose unconscionable terms only to back away once challenged. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: SAVEMUNI.COM, AN UNINCORPORATED ASSOCIATION OF et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO RECREATION AND PARK COMMISSION et al

Judge Busch will disclose that his wife, Catharine Barnes, is a deputy city attorney. She is a member of the contracts team.
